intro: Limonium aragonense, also known as the Aragonese sea lavender, is a species of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae, characterized by its blue-purple flowers and succulent leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, and treat various infections.
